title: Experts deem bill against flag destruction unconstitutional
summary: Two witnesses and a constitutional scholar noted that the regulation violates freedom of expression and lacks legal basis.

title: Fire in Dotonbori building caused by cigarette butt leaves two firefighters dead
summary: Police referred a former restaurant employee for the fire that caused the death of two firefighters, but dismissed the homicide charge.

title: Fukuoka assembly vice president denies receiving bribes
summary: Audio recordings contradict his denial, but the politician insists that no money was transferred.

title: Katsuragawa route is the preferred option for Hokuriku Shinkansen extension to Osaka
summary: The ruling party adjusts project details, though challenges persist on all proposed routes.

title: Trump announces reinstatement of naval blockade on Iran and downplays ceasefire
summary: Hostilities intensify as the ceasefire memorandum is considered inoperative and the situation deteriorates.

title: Kyoto court rejects suspension of Oi nuclear plant
summary: More than 3,000 residents had requested the shutdown due to safety risks, but the court determined there are no imminent dangers.

title: Corruption scandal at Japan Post: bribes and bid rigging over three generations
summary: The company fired three employees and a fourth person was identified, while 18 cases of bid irregularities are under investigation.

title: Japan suffers record heatwave with over 4,500 hospitalized for heatstroke
summary: Temperatures exceeded 35°C at 170 locations across the country, and media outlets launched a joint prevention campaign.

title: 'Secondary capital' bill advances in Diet with support from Mirai party
summary: The Lower House plans to approve it on July 15, but the Senate remains uncertain after the agreement on amendments between the ruling parties.

title: Bankruptcy of Zenshin credit cooperative affects Hokuriku regional banks
summary: Local banks open consultation desks and the Ministry of Economy is studying regulation for card processors, but with caution.

title: Fukuoka governor spent over 300 million yen on overseas trips in five years
summary: Expenses include luxury accommodation in Paris, sparking criticism over the use of public funds.

title: Uno Shoma and Honda Marin showcase their dance routine in open training
summary: The artistic duo aims to make an impact in the new season with an innovative and technically demanding number.

title: Nakamichi presents his vision of a 'competitive welfare state' for Japan
summary: The new opposition group seeks to counterbalance the Takaishi government with a program of social cohesion and economic efficiency.

title: Prime Minister Takaishi leads recruitment of LDP members
summary: Despite individual success, total membership in the ruling party fell for the third consecutive year.

title: US inflation moderates to 3.5% year-on-year in June
summary: The data encouraged markets, although investors remain attentive to price developments.

title: Tokyo Disneyland and DisneySea raise fees to record levels
summary: The price increase boosted service sector stocks to their highest level in 26 years.

title: Japan's finance minister urges GPIF to invest more in domestic assets
summary: The proposal divides the government, with the health minister advocating a more conservative strategy.

title: Yen weakens to 162 per dollar, pressuring Bank of Japan
summary: The currency's decline changes the outlook for a possible rate hike, as the market awaits signals.

title: International Pacific bluefin tuna summit ends without quota agreement
summary: Fishing countries failed to reach consensus on increasing catches from next year and will continue negotiations.

title: France and Spain face off in World Cup semifinals
summary: The match will determine the tournament finalist, with Spanish defense against French individual talent.
